It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | buried_individual = | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = | map = Map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | label = Kuroshima Kofun | icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest | label_position = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound

Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | buried_individual = | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = | map = Map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | label = Kuroshima Kofun | icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest | label_position = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound

{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]

To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kuroshima Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kuroshima Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[ja:黒島古墳]]

To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuroshima Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuroshima Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[ja:黒島古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]]

Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_individual = | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = | map = Map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | label = Kuroshima Kofun | icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest | label_position = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuroshima Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuroshima Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[ja:黒島古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kuroshima Kofun / Kuroshima No.1 Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the burial mound
(Central wooded area. Rear mound to the right, front mound in the back left) | alias = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 35 | latitude_sec = 54.53 | longitude_deg = 134 | longitude_min = 9 | longitude_sec = 35.45 | iso = JP-33 | shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Length: 81 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | construction_period = Early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial_facilities = Rear mound: Unknown
Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_individual = | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = | map = Map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | label = Kuroshima Kofun | icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest | label_position = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kuroshima Kofun / Kuroshima No.1 Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the burial mound
(Central wooded area. Rear mound to the right, front mound in the back left) | alias = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 35 | latitude_sec = 54.53 | longitude_deg = 134 | longitude_min = 9 | longitude_sec = 35.45 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Length: 81 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | construction_period = Early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burial_facilities = Rear mound: Unknown
Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_individual = | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = | map = Map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | label = Kuroshima Kofun | icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest | label_position = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped burial mounds in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped burial mounds surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone formal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue ware (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji ware, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite burial mound, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's scale indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue ware, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun
