The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The mound is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]][[ja:黒島古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroshima Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroshima Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroshima Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:dd to dd]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:dd to dd]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | map_dot_label = Kuroshima Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound

Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | map_dot_label = Kuroshima Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|12=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroshima Kofun}}

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|12=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|12=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{citation needed|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kuroshima Kofun / Kuroshima No.1 Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the Kofun
(Central wooded area. Rear mound to the right, front mound in the back left) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 54.53 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 35.45 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Length: 81 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | built = Early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} | burial_facilities = Rear mound: Unknown
Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | map_dot_label = Kuroshima Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|12=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{citation needed|Kuroshima Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|12=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. Construction is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{citation needed|Kuroshima Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|12=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]
