So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kuroshima Kofun / Kuroshima No.1 Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the Kofun
(Central wooded area. Rear mound to the right, front mound in the back left) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 54.53 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 35.45 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Length: 81 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | built = Early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} | burial_facilities = Rear mound: Unknown
Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | map_dot_label = Kuroshima Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|12=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound

To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|12=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. revision.text:
{{簡易区別|大分県臼杵市の黒島にある古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 黒島古墳 / 黒島1号墳 |画像 = [[File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景
（中央樹叢。右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 54.53 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 9|経度秒 = 35.45 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長81m
高さ8m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半-中葉 |埋葬施設 = 後円部：不明
前方部：[[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・陶質土器 |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Okayama |ラベル = 黒島古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-南西 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|古墳の所在する黒島（中央）}}{{small|[[牛窓オリーブ園]]から望む。画像左端の丘陵に[[牛窓天神山古墳]]が所在し、右端の丘陵跡に[[波歌山古墳]]が所在した。黒島の左は[[前島 (岡山県)|前島]]、最奥左は[[小豆島]]。}}]] {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|牛窓湾の前方後円墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=牛窓天神山 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=黒島 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=鹿歩山 | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-北東.png | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(波歌山) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=二塚山 | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-南東.png | mark-size5=25 }} '''黒島古墳'''（くろしまこふん）または'''黒島1号墳'''（くろしまいちごうふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]]の黒島にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基 !湾内位置!!古墳名!!墳丘長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |北東||[[牛窓天神山古墳]]||85m||4c中葉-後半||瀬戸内市指定史跡 |- |南||'''黒島1号墳'''||81m||5c前半-中葉||なし |- |北西||[[鹿歩山古墳]]||84m||5c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |- |北||[[波歌山古墳]]||60m||6c前半||（消滅） |- |南西||[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]||55m||6c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |}
岡山県南東部、牛窓港から南方へ約2キロメートルに浮かぶ離島の黒島において、島中央の山上最高所（標高33メートル）に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。牛窓湾では、本古墳のほか[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]・[[鹿歩山古墳]]・[[波歌山古墳]]（消滅）・[[牛窓天神山古墳]]の前方後円墳5基が湾を取り囲むように営造されたことが知られる{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。現在は墳丘上に武内神社が鎮座する{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。墳丘は2段築成と見られる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]片・[[形象埴輪]]片（蓋形・家形・人物埴輪）・[[須恵器]]（主にTK73-TK216型式期、一部TK23型式期）・[[土師器]]・陶質土器等が検出されている{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は、後円部に存在が推定されるほか（武内神社南側に推定天井石が一部露出{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}）、前方部にも[[竪穴式石室]]の盗掘坑が認められている{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|pp=82-83}}。この前方部石室は長さ2.4メートル・幅0.65メートル・高さ0.4メートルを測り{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|pp=82-83}}、内面には赤色顔料が塗られる{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。他に後円部東側にも箱形石棺があり、こちらでは[[1933年]]（[[昭和]]8年）の調査で人骨のほか鉄剣・鉄鏃・矢筒金具などが出土している{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。周辺では北側に[[陪塚]]と見られる黒島2号墳（直径10-12メートルの[[円墳]]）の築造も認められる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]前半-中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。黒島自体は本古墳の規模に見合わない小島であることから、被葬者は広く牛窓湾を基盤として活動したと推測され{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、他の前方後円墳4基とともに当時の牛窓湾の港湾としての政治的重要性を示す古墳になる{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。前代の牛窓天神山古墳とは50-80年の間が空くが、間に首長墓は認められないため、本古墳被葬者が牛窓天神山古墳被葬者の直接的後継者になるか1代空いた後継者になるかが吉備地方全体の政治動向を考察するうえでも注意される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=123-125}}。また吉備地方では陪塚を伴う古墳は[[造山古墳]]・[[両宮山古墳]]などと少ない点、当時貴重な初期須恵器を伴う点、全国的にも最古級の人物埴輪を伴う点、朝鮮半島の陶質土器を伴う点などで、ヤマト王権・吉備中枢部・朝鮮半島を間に活躍した被葬者像が示唆される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=123-125}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。 * 墳丘長：約81メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成か。 ** 直径：約48メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル ** 墳頂部平坦面直径：約14メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：約30メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成か。 ** 長さ：約36メートル ** 幅：約48メートル ** 高さ：約7メートル 前方部前端側には幅約12メートル・深さ約1.5メートルの堀切が認められる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。 File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂
{{small|武内神社が鎮座。}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む。 File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|前方部墳頂
{{small|手前に陥没孔と露出石材。}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む。 File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|黒島2号墳 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=黒島1号墳」「黒島2号墳|title=牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|pages=138-150|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=|title=牛窓町史 通史編|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 通史編|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=黒島1号墳|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639 |title=牛窓町古墳図|publisher=牛窓町教育委員会|isbn=|pages=82-83|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町古墳図|2002}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=黒島古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=黒島1号墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=黒島古墳|title=岡山県史 一八 考古資料|publisher=岡山県|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろしまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[黒島古墳]] to [[Kuroshima Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

墳丘全景
（中央樹叢。右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 54.53 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 9|経度秒 = 35.45 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長81m
高さ8m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半-中葉 |埋葬施設 = 後円部：不明
前方部：[[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・陶質土器 |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Okayama |ラベル = 黒島古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-南西 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|古墳の所在する黒島（中央）}}{{small|[[牛窓オリーブ園]]から望む。画像左端の丘陵に[[牛窓天神山古墳]]が所在し、右端の丘陵跡に[[波歌山古墳]]が所在した。黒島の左は[[前島 (岡山県)|前島]]、最奥左は[[小豆島]]。}}]] {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|牛窓湾の前方後円墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=牛窓天神山 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=黒島 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=鹿歩山 | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-北東.png | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(波歌山) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=二塚山 | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-南東.png | mark-size5=25 }} '''黒島古墳'''（くろしまこふん）または'''黒島1号墳'''（くろしまいちごうふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]]の黒島にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基 !湾内位置!!古墳名!!墳丘長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |北東||[[牛窓天神山古墳]]||85m||4c中葉-後半||瀬戸内市指定史跡 |- |南||'''黒島1号墳'''||81m||5c前半-中葉||なし |- |北西||[[鹿歩山古墳]]||84m||5c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |- |北||[[波歌山古墳]]||60m||6c前半||（消滅） |- |南西||[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]||55m||6c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |}
岡山県南東部、牛窓港から南方へ約2キロメートルに浮かぶ離島の黒島において、島中央の山上最高所（標高33メートル）に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。牛窓湾では、本古墳のほか[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]・[[鹿歩山古墳]]・[[波歌山古墳]]（消滅）・[[牛窓天神山古墳]]の前方後円墳5基が湾を取り囲むように営造されたことが知られる{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。現在は墳丘上に武内神社が鎮座する{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。墳丘は2段築成と見られる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]片・[[形象埴輪]]片（蓋形・家形・人物埴輪）・[[須恵器]]（主にTK73-TK216型式期、一部TK23型式期）・[[土師器]]・陶質土器等が検出されている{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は、後円部に存在が推定されるほか（武内神社南側に推定天井石が一部露出{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}）、前方部にも[[竪穴式石室]]の盗掘坑が認められている{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|pp=82-83}}。この前方部石室は長さ2.4メートル・幅0.65メートル・高さ0.4メートルを測り{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|pp=82-83}}、内面には赤色顔料が塗られる{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。他に後円部東側にも箱形石棺があり、こちらでは[[1933年]]（[[昭和]]8年）の調査で人骨のほか鉄剣・鉄鏃・矢筒金具などが出土している{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。周辺では北側に[[陪塚]]と見られる黒島2号墳（直径10-12メートルの[[円墳]]）の築造も認められる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]前半-中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。黒島自体は本古墳の規模に見合わない小島であることから、被葬者は広く牛窓湾を基盤として活動したと推測され{{Sfn|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、他の前方後円墳4基とともに当時の牛窓湾の港湾としての政治的重要性を示す古墳になる{{Sfn|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}。前代の牛窓天神山古墳とは50-80年の間が空くが、間に首長墓は認められないため、本古墳被葬者が牛窓天神山古墳被葬者の直接的後継者になるか1代空いた後継者になるかが吉備地方全体の政治動向を考察するうえでも注意される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=123-125}}。また吉備地方では陪塚を伴う古墳は[[造山古墳]]・[[両宮山古墳]]などと少ない点、当時貴重な初期須恵器を伴う点、全国的にも最古級の人物埴輪を伴う点、朝鮮半島の陶質土器を伴う点などで、ヤマト王権・吉備中枢部・朝鮮半島を間に活躍した被葬者像が示唆される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=123-125}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。 * 墳丘長：約81メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成か。 ** 直径：約48メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル ** 墳頂部平坦面直径：約14メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：約30メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成か。 ** 長さ：約36メートル ** 幅：約48メートル ** 高さ：約7メートル 前方部前端側には幅約12メートル・深さ約1.5メートルの堀切が認められる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=138-150}}。 File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂
{{small|武内神社が鎮座。}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む。 File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|前方部墳頂
{{small|手前に陥没孔と露出石材。}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む。 File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|黒島2号墳 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=黒島1号墳」「黒島2号墳|title=牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|pages=138-150|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=|title=牛窓町史 通史編|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 通史編|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=黒島1号墳|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639 |title=牛窓町古墳図|publisher=牛窓町教育委員会|isbn=|pages=82-83|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町古墳図|2002}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=黒島古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|黒島古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=黒島1号墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|黒島1号墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=黒島古墳|title=岡山県史 一八 考古資料|publisher=岡山県|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろしまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|qq=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|qq=draft}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]

Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]

Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|qq=}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shodoshima|qq=}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]]

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|qq=}}, with [[:en:Shōdoshima|Shodoshima]] visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. Rear mound to the right, front mound in the back left) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 54.53 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 35.45 | iso = JP-33 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length: 81 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | built = Early to mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burial_facilities = Rear mound: Unknown
Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | map_dot_label = Kuroshima Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|qq=}}, with [[:en:Shōdoshima|Shodoshima]] visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]] [[Category:岡山県の古墳]]

The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]]

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|qq=}}, with [[:en:Shōdoshima|Shodoshima]] visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|qq=}}, with [[:en:Shōdoshima|Shodoshima]] visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-shaped stone coffin on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kuroshima Kofun]] [[ja:黒島古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Kuroshima Kofun}}
