The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|1=Mae-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397116}}, with [[:en:Shōdoshima|Shodoshima]] visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-type Sarcophagus on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]]. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{Commons category|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28689542}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kuroshima Kofun / Kuroshima No.1 Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the Kofun
(Central wooded area. Rear mound to the right, front mound in the back left) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.598481|N|134.159847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length: 81 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | built = Early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} | burial_facilities = Rear mound: Unknown
Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | map_dot_label = Kuroshima Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|1=Mae-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397116}}, with {{ill|Shōdoshima|en|Shōdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=Shodoshima}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-type Sarcophagus on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{Commons category|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28689542}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|1=Mae-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397116}}, with {{ill|Shōdoshima|en|Shōdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=Shodoshima}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-type Sarcophagus on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{Commons category|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28689542}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|1=Mae-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397116}}, with {{ill|Shōdoshima|en|Shōdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=Shodoshima|1=Shōdoshima}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-type Sarcophagus on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|1=Mae-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397116}}, with {{ill|Shōdoshima|en|Shōdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=Shodoshima|1=Shōdoshima}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-type Sarcophagus on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{Commons category|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28689542}} [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

View of the Kofun
(Central wooded area. Rear mound to the right, front mound in the back left) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570212}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.598481|N|134.159847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Length: 81 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | built = Early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | burial_facilities = Rear mound: Unknown
Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | map_dot_label = Kuroshima Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|1=Mae-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11397116}}, with {{ill|Shōdoshima|en|Shōdoshima|qid=Q2166413|lt=Shodoshima|1=Shōdoshima}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570212}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-type Sarcophagus on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{Commons category|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28689542}} [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Front mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, ceramic ware | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = map of Okayama Prefecture Ja.svg | map_dot_label = Kuroshima Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The Kofun's location on Kuroshima (center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. The hill to the far left contains {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and the remnants of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} are on the right. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|1=Mae-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11397116}}, with {{ill|Shōdoshima|en|Shōdoshima|qid=Q2166413|lt=Shodoshima|1=Shōdoshima}} visible in the far background.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Kuroshima Kofun''' (黒島古墳) or '''Kuroshima No.1 Kofun''' (黒島1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located on Kuroshima Island in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570212}}. The mound is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Kuroshima Kofun was built atop the highest point (33 meters above sea level) on Kuroshima, a small island approximately 2 kilometers south of Ushimado Port. It is one of five keyhole-shaped Kofuns surrounding Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. A shrine, Takeuchi Jinja, now sits atop the mound, which has not undergone typeal excavation. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front section facing southwest. It is believed to have a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical and figurative haniwa (including lid-shaped, house-shaped, and human haniwa), Sue pottery (primarily of the TK73-TK216 style, with some TK23), Haji pottery, and ceramic ware have been recovered from the mound surface. While burial facilities are inferred to exist in the rear mound, traces of a plundered vertical stone chamber are evident in the front mound. This front chamber measures 2.4 meters in length, 0.65 meters in width, and 0.4 meters in height, with traces of red pigment on its interior walls. Additionally, a box-type Sarcophagus on the eastern side of the rear mound yielded human remains, iron swords, arrowheads, and quiver fittings during a 1933 investigation. Nearby, a possible satellite Kofun, Kuroshima No.2 Kofun, has been identified as a 10-12 meter circular tomb. built is estimated to have occurred in the early to mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. Despite the island's small size, the mound's map_size indicates the interment of an individual who wielded considerable influence over Ushimado Bay, underscoring the bay's political significance. The tomb is notable for accompanying one of the earliest known examples of human-shaped haniwa, Korean-style ceramics, and rare early Sue pottery, suggesting connections between the Yamato court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 81 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Flat top diameter: Approximately 14 meters * Narrowing section: ** Width: Approximately 30 meters * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 36 meters ** Width: Approximately 48 meters ** Height: Approximately 7 meters The front end of the mound features a trench approximately 12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, funcho.jpg|Flat top of the rear mound
{{small|Takeuchi Shrine occupies this space.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, kouenbu-2.jpg|View of the rear mound from the front mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Flat top of the front mound
{{small|Collapsed areas and exposed stonework are visible.}} File:Kuroshima Kofun-1, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View of the front mound from the rear mound File:Kuroshima Kofun-2, funcho.jpg|Kuroshima No.2 Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒島古墳|version=97480462|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:黒島古墳]] {{Commons category|Kuroshima Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q28689542}} [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
